entry.title: Re: New Asset Creating Warning Message- Message No-AU176
entry.content:
1. You MUST read the long text of the message
  2. If and when correct, then deactivate the message: Asset Accounting -> Transactions -> Maintain Message Types

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Font-Style for iOS - change Font size?
entry.content: Can you give an example control where you are trying to change the font size?

entry.title: Re: SAP Dynamic Forms 1.0 Released Features
entry.content:
Hi @WilliamChou, 

In the blog it states "Standalone usage of Forms via Fiori interface is planned for 2.0 release (Create Maintenance Request Find Maintenance Notification, Find Maintenance Order)"

Does this mean that the full functionality of forms will be in Fiori, e.g forms linked to work order operations or task list, or will users always need SAAM to use forms in this way?

Many thanks for the information,

David

entry.title: Re: Mass Fixed Asset Retirement OData Version issue
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: REFX Infinite Building (perpetual term): contract mas...
entry.content:
Hi @LiLing, I'm a bit lost with your question. What we really want to achieve: the same calculation result compared to the Excel formula with a quarterly payment =-PV(quarterly discount rate; 4 quarters * 999 years;quarterly payment;;1). In such case, the periodic liability clearing would be equal the periodic interest.

Is there a way to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: Learn How to create a Support Case - SAP for Me
entry.content: Is it possible to categories and cluster together a list of versions created in SAC by year and month?

entry.title: Re: expected status code 200 but got 500: "error creating...
entry.content: Please see 3236427 - Mass transfer ID XXX is already being used to replicate data for table XXX - SAP for Me

entry.title: Where can I start learning features and functions of SAP Sustainability Data Exchange?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange learning journey.

Overview
The learners will be able to use SAP Sustainability Data Exchange to collect, manage and share the product carbon footprint data with business partners across the supply chain.

Learning objectives

  • Explain the business challenges of supply chain sustainability
  • Maintain the master data including business partners and products
  • Request, manage and share product carbon footprint data 
Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Processing exception during a "Render" operation. .Pr...
entry.content:
Dear AnurodhSrivastava,

please create a support ticket with the component BC-SRV-FP 

 

Regards,

Sandra

entry.title: Standard Prepayment process in SAP ERP 6.0
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have completed the functional configuration for the standard prepayment process. However, I am not confident about how the SD pricing condition types AZWA, AZWB, and AZDI are functioning. Could someone please clarify how these conditions are expected to behave in the sales order, pro forma/down payment invoice, and final invoice?

For example:- I created a sales order and saved it, but noticed that the pricing condition types AZWA, AZWB, and AZDI are appearing with zero value. I then tried to create a pro forma/down payment invoice (FAZ), and again observed that these condition types are showing as zero. I received the payment using transaction code F-29 for the above pro forma/down payment invoice.

Subsequently, I proceeded to create the delivery and final invoice (F2), but once more, the pricing condition types AZWA, AZWB, and AZDI were displayed with zero value. attached the screenshot for reference.

Could someone kindly share their expertise and provide guidance on this process? Am I missing something or doing anything incorrectly?

Best regards,

Subhramanyu

entry.title: Re: RAP - create child with IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE
entry.content:
Hi, 
i had the same problem today and the solution is under :
https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/fc4c71aa50014fd1b43721701471913d/fd45f76da7ab49bd8a6ef94a4b64f340.html
if you have any questions please let me know 🙂
best regards

entry.title: Re: DM and STC integration - creation of a datasource in...
entry.content:
Hello @a_migliore 

 

See help:


https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DISCLOSURE_MANAGEMENT/6fda503523d6413597f5cd81be59fa8a/e59d60949549424a91a515c46b1f570f.html 

Known Limitations
The data source can only be created for the server.

 

So - make sure you have a server connection, and then you can create the new source

Regards,

Marc

entry.title: Re: CAP Fiori Elements warnings on READ requests
entry.content: Hi! Did you find any information why it doesn't work?

entry.title: Re: Create By Association for 1:1 composition in RAP Unma...
entry.content:
Hi, 
i had the same problem today and the solution is under :
https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/fc4c71aa50014fd1b43721701471913d/fd45f76da7ab49bd8a6ef94...
best regards

entry.title: Organisational Data in SAP FSM
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I hope this message finds you well.

We are currently working on an integration between SAP Field Service Management (FSM) and SAP S/4HANA Service using SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI).

On the S/4HANA side, we have a comprehensive and well-structured service organization hierarchy. Our objective is to leverage this existing organizational structure within SAP FSM—not only at the company level but also to enforce visibility restrictions across different service units. For instance, we want to ensure that dispatchers and technicians can only access service calls and data relevant to their specific unit.

Is there a recommended approach or best practice to replicate or integrate this type of organizational hierarchy in FSM to support such restrictions?

Any insights or suggestions would be highly appreciated.

Best regards,

entry.title: Sequential Multicast suppressing error details
entry.content:
When an OData calls, after a multicast step (a single multicast step, not nested), the error details are suppressed when sending to Exception Handling.

i.e. OData Error

<error>

<code>SLS_LORD/033</code>

<message>The document is locked</message>

</error>

Error available in Exception Handling:

org.apache.camel.CamelExchangeException: Multicast processing failed for number 0. Exchange[55EE78BA9D8B2F2-0000000000000004]. Caused by: [com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException - Bad Request : 400 : HTTP/1.1 ]

 

How do we get to the OData error in our error handling? The normal Groovy script can only return the Camel error, which is the Multicast processing error. It can't retrieve the OData error

SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Re: Standard Prepayment process in SAP ERP 6.0
entry.content:
Did you already read

  • Blogs such as SD Condition Based Down Payments 
  • KBAs such as 3309172 - In condition based down payment scenario, even if the order is fully delivered, the condition value of condition type AZWB is 0 in invoice or 3031123 - Unable to adjust the condition value for condition type AZWR in invoice which contains down payment clearing item

entry.title: Re: User-exit to Add partner functions in Partners tab of VA01/VA02 that gets triggered at item leve
entry.content:
Hello, I am facing a similar issue. Can you please advise what is in your method get_partner in class:

Class /ebos/so_cl_o2vmapping

entry.title: Analysis for Office – Planning data not saved after system upgrade
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm facing a strange issue after a recent upgrade of our SAP system to S/4HANA 2023.

We have a planning query that is used in SAP Analysis for Office (AfO), and it has always worked fine. The report opens and refreshes correctly, and the input-ready cells are still editable as expected. However, after we enter planning values and click "Save Data", the system reverts the values back to the previous ones — nothing is saved.

Could you help me?

 

Thanks

Best Regards

entry.title: successfactors api JobApplicationAssessmentOrder update
entry.content:
Hi

I'm using the api  JobApplicationAssessmentOrder to get the assessment orders but the api hub offers only GET. Is there an api available to update the assessmentOrders using put or post method.

entry.content:
Hello for EHS module, i need translation in french and spanish witch are missing. 

I fund that i have to consult sap note 1146700, but i can't fing it. 

Do you know where i could found any information about it ? 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Create PDF with CONVERT_OTF in Unicode system
entry.content: Thanks - this fixed the problem of mangled output!

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content: I love the part where I came to this blog and read it several times and went to a bunch of other articles and links in SAP with lots of stuff to read. I thought I was just going to click on a link to a .dmg file and start working. Instead I got to read a whole bunch of stuff about stuff I don't know. It would have been disappointing if someone just put a link to a .dmg file. Then, I would have to go to work and couldn't waste my day scrolling around.

entry.title: Re: ASP.NET Report Viewer & multiple IIS Worker Processes
entry.content: R&D is wondering what SP you upgraded from? They say nothing has changed in this area...

entry.title: Why can't I configure Generative AI Hub in AI Launch pad?
entry.content:
Hi, I am following the Developer mission below for setting SAP Gen AI hub in the AI launchpad. However, I can't enable my pre-requisite service plans for AI core (Extended) and AI launchpad (Standard). I can configure AI launchpad with Free tier plan only. 

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/protected/index.html#/mymissiondetail/155262/?tab=projectboard

I am using Pay as You go BTP account and Free tier plan for AI launchpad to configure this, but not sure this can be achieved only with Enterprise accounts. Can you please suggest on how to enable Gen AI in Launchpad?

entry.title: Where can I learn features and requirements of advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP) in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring aATP in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey discusses essential features and requirements of advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP) in SAP S/4HANA. It provides a detailed overview how to setup and use these functionalities. Checking Methods in Detail and Advanced ATP in SAP S/4HANA are discussed.Also the concept and use of Backorder Processing is explained. In addition, both Supply Based Confirmation (SBC) and Business Process Scheduling (BPS) are covered. With system demo recordings and extensive hands-on exercises, you will gain a thorough understanding of how aATP can support your logistics processes.

Learning objectives
By the end of this learning journey, you will be able to describe and execute business processes in SAP S/4HANA aATP.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring SAP S/4HANA Sales
Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Query Help - Onhand Inventory Date in Warehouse
entry.content:
Hello,


The main table that you need to search is OILM - Inventory Log Message Table

 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Can you use CurrentSalary and meritTarget in the same...
entry.content:
Hi @jennifer_mckellar 

 

I believe only merit (meritTarget) and lumpsum (lumpsumTarget) have target fields hence can be marked as basedOn=Target but I will double check in implementation guide and with product team and confirm here.

All the best, 

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected durin...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

There are any approval procedure active in your system?

Best regards

entry.title: Join Our Webinar on the Future and Evolution of SAP IQ
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced world, staying competitive means staying ahead of the curve in data management. If you're running your database technology on-premises, you might be facing challenges in scalability, agility, and cost-efficiency. The time for change is now, and SAP IQ, with its rich history, is evolving to meet these challenges head-on.

We are excited to invite you to our enlightening webinar, "The Future and Evolution of SAP IQ," taking place on April 29th at 11 AM EDT. This event is designed to help you understand the current landscape and explore the transformative benefits of migrating your SAP IQ database to the cloud.

 

Why Should You Attend?

  1. Understand the Legacy and Impact of SAP IQ in Data Management: Dive into the rich history of SAP IQ and its profound impact on data management within the SAP ecosystem.
  2. Learn About the Latest SAP IQ Release: Stay updated with the latest release strategies and features that SAP IQ offers to empower your data management practices.
  3. Explore the Tangible Benefits of Cloud Database Modernization and Migration: Discover how migrating to SAP HANA Cloud, data lake relational engine, can provide enhanced scalability, increased agility, cost savings, and improved data security and compliance.
  4. Discover Practical Steps and Support Resources for a Successful Modernization Journey: Get insights into best practices and strategic approaches for modernizing your SAP IQ environment. Learn about the comprehensive support available to ensure a seamless transition and optimal performance in the cloud.

Who Should Attend?

This webinar is a must-attend for:

  • Data Professionals: Gain insight into innovative strategies for data management.
  • IT Managers: Learn about practical steps to modernize your database environment.
  • Business Leaders: Understand the business value and competitive advantages of leveraging cloud-based solutions.

Key Takeaways:

  • Comprehensive Insight: Understand the legacy and future of SAP IQ.
  • Latest Information: Stay informed about the latest SAP IQ release.
  • Strategic Benefits: Learn about the transformative benefits of cloud database modernization.
  • Practical Steps: Gain practical insights and access to support resources for a successful modernization journey.

Join us to unlock the full potential of SAP HANA Cloud and drive your organization into the future of data management. Don't miss this opportunity to elevate your data strategy to new heights!

Webinar Details:

  • Topic: The Future and Evolution of SAP IQ
  • Date: April 29th
  • Time: 11 AM EDT

Register Now to ask questions of the experts and take a step towards the future of data management. We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: Re: Looking for feedback: Identity and Access Management resources (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content: Registration has ended. Let me know in case you still want to sign up

entry.title: Capturing Pricing with 4 Decimal Places in Sales Orders
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I need assistance with the following requirement related to pricing in sales orders.

In our project, we are using manual pricing conditions because the pricing data is received from an external, non-SAP system. This system maintains prices with four decimal places (e.g., 1.2345). However, standard SAP only supports two decimal places, which causes the system to round the price to 1.23 when entered manually.

Our requirement is to capture the price exactly as received from the external system, without any rounding or truncation.

Current Scenario:

  • Non-SAP system price: 1.2345

  • SAP captured price: 1.23

Required Outcome:

  • Non-SAP system price: 1.2345

  • SAP captured price: 1.2345

Is there a way to enable or configure SAP to support four decimal places in pricing conditions?

Looking forward to your suggestions.
Thank you!

entry.title: Re: ASP.NET Report Viewer & multiple IIS Worker Processes
entry.content: Hi, it was 13_0_24 but I was running 32 bit, the App Pool had "Enable 32 bit" set to True, but when I installed 13_0_37 I couldn't get 32 bit to work (despite having the runtime installed) so I changed the project & App pool to 64 bit - therefore I'm not sure if it's the change in version as such or the change in bitness. It would be great if they could give an indication into what can be checked/tested. I still think the Client prompted parameter must/should be within the viewstate/postback? I have a machine key within the web.config file (I believe it's used with multiple worker processes) but maybe that's not being picked up correctly for some reason. Any help greatly appreciated! Thanks

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content:
The only Events available in the WEB viewer are these":

I've asked R&D about making these types of events available in the WEB viewer and the problem is the view of the report is EPF based, it's an image of each page. There isn't an easy way to allow the browser and the viewer to talk to each other.

What you can do though it add a subreport to the main report so if you want another report link the subreport to the new report, you can then simply drill down.

Use RAS to add subreports in code.

See this page for the Reference material:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CRYSTAL_REPORTS,_DEVELOPER_VERSION_FOR_MICROSOFT_VISUAL_STUDIO?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Is output print preview button available in Manage Sa...
entry.content: this button is not available in V2 app, we did enhancement to achieve this requirement

I get my BOM back but I don't have the hierarchy. Do you have an idea please ?

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Object table Single Select mode instead of mu...
entry.content:
Can you elaborate on the scenario?  If you want only one selection you don't need to go into multiple select just trigger your action in the OnPress of the Object cell rather than trying to show a secondary checkbox and requiring an additional press on a button to execute.

If you really need the secondary press you can use either an accessory image or icon stack image to show the selected item and manage it through your metadata.

entry.title: Re: No-Code Guide: SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Build Apps Data Visibility
entry.content: Good morning. Quick question, how can I make a project have more than one member? Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: Hi Don!

entry.title: Re: WILD CARD char in file Name
entry.content:
Try below

DATA: BEGIN OF lt_tab OCCURS 0,
          line(225) TYPE c,
      END OF lt_tab,
      lv_command(225) TYPE c.
CONCATENATE 'ls -lt ' lv_path '2005*' INTO lv_command.
CALL 'SYSTEM' ID 'COMMAND' FIELD lv_command ID 'TAB' FIELD lt_tab-*sys*.
IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
 SORT lt_tab.
ENDIF.

  Should get 2005stcjk.txt file

entry.title: Re: Can you use CurrentSalary and meritTarget in the same...
entry.content:
@jennifer_mckellar 

Confirming that only lumpsumTarget, lumpsum2Target and meritTarget are supported.

Maybe switch extra around with one of the lumpsum fields ?

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: Hi Don!, It will be really helpful if DoublePageClick is javascript event or something that we can handle, so it can return e.ObjectInfos. Because I can send a Callback to the server by ASPxCallback (DevExpress callback) or Ajax callback and send objectinfo that I need then open another report after ajax is complete. but unfortunately DoublePageClick doens't exist in Web version. You can support this event as Callback event or something. so after callback is complete I can take e.ObjectInfo I need and open external report based on that...

entry.title: Re: When Job Requisition is in filled state it is not sho...
entry.content: Could you please help me with the rule that needs to be configured. Is this state the state that the rule should be created based on to be Hired field (which will work from user's DOJ) which also means the job requisition will not be displayed in position org chart and it provide access to create another requisition or the sentence "if the position is marked as occupied (which only happens once the candidate is hired in EC), then the system should not allow the creation of a new requisition against that position" states that we can create and avoid creation of requisition before their DOJ?

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: I don't actually use DrillDown, But I open external report based on e.ObjectInfos parameters after double click on Row. for example opening Statement of Account report based on current selection account from Balances Report see following video please to describe my scenario: https://youtu.be/e_f8ntBeI-w

entry.title: Re: ASP.NET Report Viewer & multiple IIS Worker Processes
entry.content: Yes, on my Dev PC everything seemed ok but on runtime server, I ran the two separate installs for 32 bit and 64 bit but 32 bit gave a Class error (dont have the specific error now but could probably reproduce it if helpful) I tried lots of things but in the end decided to move to 64 bit

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content:
As I noted, DEV can't add any more events due to limitations in the Viewer and permissions etc. As a work around add a blank subreport in the section required, then use the DrillDownSubreport event to redirect your app to open a new report in a new tab based on your logic. Asking them for an enhancement request will take time if they agree to do it or if it's possible...

Using Java to do this should work though, I recall a while ago this was the solution but I don't recall how to now...

The Desktop Viewer does have a Double Click row Event but that's not going to help you....

entry.title: Choosing Between Knowledge Graphs and Property Graphs in SAP HANA Cloud and Why Both Matter
entry.content:
Introduction:

In Q1 2025, SAP introduced the Knowledge Graph Engine as a powerful addition to SAP HANA Cloud’s multi-model capabilities, bringing RDF triples, SPARQL support, and semantic reasoning into the same platform that already houses relational, JSON, vector, spatial, and graph data. In my previous blog, I shared an overview of what this new engine means for businesses looking to connect and contextualize their data, especially in use cases like grounding GenAI responses or enabling smarter applications.

But this isn’t the first graph capability SAP HANA Cloud supports. The Property Graph Engine in SAP HANA Cloud has long been a powerful tool for running graph algorithms, path-based queries, and analytics over entity relationships. Now that both engines are available side by side, naturally, the next question is:

When should I use the Knowledge Graph Engine, and when does the Property Graph Engine make more sense?

In this blog, I will walk through their differences, typical use cases, and where each engine shines individually, and even more so when used together. I will also bring in examples from supply chain scenarios to illustrate how businesses can benefit from both types of graph models, depending on their data and goals.

Understanding the Two Graph Engines in SAP HANA Cloud

Before diving into when to use which engine, let us quickly recap what sets the Property Graph Engine and the newly added Knowledge Graph Engine apart. Both help storing and analyzing connected data, but they serve different purposes and use different structures to get there.

Property Graph Engine

SAP HANA Cloud’s Property Graph Engine represents data as nodes (entities) and edges (relationships), with both capable of storing key-value properties. This model is especially powerful for running graph algorithms like shortest path, centrality, or community detection. If you are analyzing how entities are related and want to optimize paths or discover clusters, property graphs are ideal.

Example:
In a supply chain, you can use a property graph to model how products move through suppliers, factories, and distribution centers, and run algorithms to detect potential bottlenecks or key risk nodes.

Knowledge Graph Engine

The Knowledge Graph Engine follows a semantic model, storing facts as triples: subject-predicate-object (the Resource Description Framework or RDF format). It focuses on meaning, inference, and integration, using standards such as RDF, SPARQL, RDFS, and OWL. This model is useful when you want to enrich data with context, bring in external vocabularies, and perform reasoning across linked entities.

Example:
In the same supply chain, a knowledge graph helps answer more contextual questions:
"Which suppliers provide eco-certified packaging for products sold in EU countries with plastic regulations?" With ontologies and semantic rules, you can define what "eco-certified" means and build queries that go beyond simple connections.

When to Use What: Choosing Between Property Graphs and Knowledge Graphs

Let us now walk through some common business needs and map them to the right graph engine.

Use the Property Graph Engine when:

  • You need graph algorithms like shortest path, centrality, or clustering.
  • Your focus is on analyzing structural relationships (not meaning or semantics).
  • You want to perform fast traversals over large networks of connected data.
  • Your graph data is relational in nature (e.g., stored in SQL tables or JSON).

Example:
In a supply chain network, you may want to analyze how disruptions spread. You model factories, suppliers, and routes as nodes, and use Louvain community detection to identify clusters of suppliers that are tightly interconnected. If one supplier fails, you can quickly assess the ripple effect across the network.

Use the Knowledge Graph Engine when:

  • You need semantic reasoning or integration across diverse datasets.
  • Your queries are contextual: based on meaning, not just structure.
  • You want to enforce or leverage ontologies and linked vocabularies.
  • You are working with federated data, possibly involving external sources.
  • You are building apps with GenAI and domain-specific context.

Example:
In the same supply chain, you might want to find:
"Which suppliers follow certified sustainable sourcing practices, provide biodegradable packaging, and deliver products classified as eco-friendly under EU regulations?"

This question requires not only linking internal data but also understanding what “sustainable” means, which can vary depending on standards, suppliers, and regions. Using RDF triples, SPARQL, and an ontology of sustainability concepts, the Knowledge Graph Engine can help with storing and querying this rich, connected view of your business.

Complementary Strengths: Why You Might Want Both

While each engine serves a different purpose, many real-world scenarios benefit from using property graphs and knowledge graphs together. SAP HANA Cloud enables this capability by allowing both engines to operate on the same platform, accessing shared data, supporting hybrid queries, and powering intelligent applications.

Let us revisit our supply chain example.

Imagine you are building an application to support:

  • Operational insights (e.g., where are my logistics risks?)
  • Compliance monitoring (e.g., sustainability checks)
  • Interactive assistant queries (e.g., “Which suppliers are compliant and unaffected by current delays?”)

To meet these needs:

  • Use the Property Graph Engine to model shipment routes, supplier nodes, and run graph analytics like route optimization or delay propagation.
  • Use the Knowledge Graph Engine to add semantic facts, such as certifications, compliance rules, and environmental impacts, using ontologies and SPARQL.

Together, these graphs form a complete picture:

  • The property graph helps you understand how things move and interact.
  • The knowledge graph tells you why those things matter and what they mean.

Instead of choosing between these two powerful models, you can combine both for richer, multi-layered insights, especially when powering GenAI use cases that require both structured relationships and contextual meaning. SAP HANA Cloud makes this possible within a single system, reducing integration overhead while enabling AI-ready, semantically rich applications that scale with your business.

Conclusion and What’s Next

With the addition of the Knowledge Graph Engine, SAP HANA Cloud brings both structural analytics and semantic intelligence to one unified, scalable platform. Whether you are analyzing complex networks or enriching enterprise data with meaning and reasoning, you now have the right tools for the job.

To sum it up:

  • Use property graphs for structure, speed, and graph computation
  • Use knowledge graphs for meaning, standards, and semantic context
  • Combine both for next-generation apps, powered by GenAI and business logic

Get Started

  • If you missed it, read the introduction to the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine here.
  • Prefer listening? Check out my recent SAP BTP podcast talk where I discuss the Knowledge Graph Engine, real use cases, and how it fits into SAP HANA Cloud’s broader multi-model story.
  • Explore the SPARQL_TABLE, SPARQL_EXECUTE, SPARQL reference guide and the Graph Workspace capabilities in SAP HANA Cloud.
  • Try building a hybrid use case with both engines, such as linking shipment delay risks to supplier compliance data.

I will continue to share more updates, patterns, and real-world use cases around SAP HANA Cloud’s multi-model capabilities in upcoming posts. Follow me here on the SAP Community to stay in the loop, and feel free to share your own experiences or questions below.

entry.title: Re: Partial packing of TO line item in RF transaction LM46
entry.content: Hi Jeff,

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud, embedded edition – the Way Ahead
entry.content:
Hi @MKreitlein, @SaikrishnaBollineni 

Do we have any evidence from SAP where they have announced this? So far I've only heard rumors and SAC Embedded is part of S/4HANA, which has nothing to do with BDC for now?

entry.title: Re: MDK - How to change the AccessoryType Icon on Grid ta...
entry.content: Correct. You would track the current state in page client data or if you have a field on the entity you could update as well and the use that state tracker to determine which image to show.

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!
entry.content: Hi Wolfang. Thanks for trying. I did go through your process however when scheduling the exam i have the following message "You have reached the attempts limit or your subscription has expired. Please renew your subscription at SAP Training and Certification Shop

entry.title: Add ALV on my report
entry.content:
Hello Community , 

i have report with list export and i want to add ALV Grid when i execute the report.

How can i  do this ?

My code is :

REPORT zget_customer_texts_list.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_text,
kunnr TYPE kunnr,
bukrs TYPE bukrs,
tdname TYPE thead-tdname,
language TYPE sylangu,
tdline TYPE tline-tdline,
END OF ty_text.

DATA: lt_result TYPE TABLE OF ty_text,
ls_result TYPE ty_text,
lt_text_lines TYPE TABLE OF tline,
ls_text_line TYPE tline,
lv_tdname TYPE thead-tdname,
lv_kunnr_conv TYPE kunnr,
lv_language TYPE sylangu.

SELECT-OPTIONS: s_kunnr FOR lv_kunnr_conv,
s_bukrs FOR ls_result-bukrs.

START-OF-SELECTION.

LOOP AT s_kunnr INTO DATA(ls_kunnr).
lv_kunnr_conv = ls_kunnr-low.

" ALPHA conversion 
CALL FUNCTION 'CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_OUTPUT'
EXPORTING
input = lv_kunnr_conv
IMPORTING
output = lv_kunnr_conv.

LOOP AT s_bukrs INTO DATA(ls_bukrs).

CLEAR: lt_text_lines.

CONCATENATE '00' lv_kunnr_conv ls_bukrs-low INTO lv_tdname.


SELECT SINGLE tdspras INTO lv_language
FROM stxh
WHERE tdobject = 'KNB1'
AND tdid = 'ZA01'
AND tdname = lv_tdname.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
lv_language = 'G'. " Greek
ENDIF.


CALL FUNCTION 'READ_TEXT'
EXPORTING
id = 'ZA01'
language = lv_language
name = lv_tdname
object = 'KNB1'
TABLES
lines = lt_text_lines
EXCEPTIONS
not_found = 1
others = 2.

IF sy-subrc = 0.
LOOP AT lt_text_lines INTO ls_text_line.
ls_result-kunnr = lv_kunnr_conv.
ls_result-bukrs = ls_bukrs-low.
ls_result-tdname = lv_tdname.
ls_result-language = lv_language.
ls_result-tdline = ls_text_line-tdline.
APPEND ls_result TO lt_result.
ENDLOOP.
ELSE.


CLEAR ls_result.
ls_result-kunnr = lv_kunnr_conv.
ls_result-bukrs = ls_bukrs-low.
ls_result-tdname = lv_tdname.
ls_result-language = lv_language.
ls_result-tdline = '--- No Text Found ---'.
APPEND ls_result TO lt_result.
ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.
ENDLOOP.

" Εμφάνιση σε λίστα
IF lt_result IS INITIAL.
WRITE: / 'Δεν βρέθηκαν κείμενα.'.
ELSE.
WRITE: / 'Customer Texts:'.
SKIP.

LOOP AT lt_result INTO ls_result.
WRITE: / 'BP:', ls_result-kunnr,
'BUKRS:', ls_result-bukrs,
'LANG:', ls_result-language,
'TEXT:', ls_result-tdline.
ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: CISI (Correction of installed software information) xml Update using SUM
entry.content:
Thank you Mukhesh, just one thing to add that, change doesn't reflect immediately it takes time. So if you finish your SUM process and immediately check the status it won't be there.

It works with both SUM1.0 and 2.0, I finished mine with 2.0.

Regards,

DK

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!
entry.content: One more thing: Do not spend your own money on the subscription. Make sure you have a valid Universal ID @ Account.sap.com and then get a Hub subscription @ https://training.sap.com/LearningHubInternalEdition . The test is free of charge as part of subscription

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: I understand, but I did say the page is a picture of the report so there is no way to detect which object of the actual report your are double clicking on and there no way to detect the column the user clicked on... By using Drill down or a subreport will allow the report to open another report(subreport). I pinged Dell and Ido to help you with the design.

entry.title: Re: Fiori RAP: Define Sort Order for Value Help
entry.content:
Hi @ivan_kalcha,

In the Metadata Extension of the CDS View Value Help you could try adding the following annotation in the header section:

@ui.presentationVariant:[{ sortOrder: [{ by: '[FieldName]', direction: #ASC }] }]
annotate entity [CDSValueHelpName] with { ... }

In case the field you want to sort is of type CHAR, you can try casting to INT (if this does not work as expected or is not permitted, try casting to NUMC then to INT) as the following:

cast( cast([FieldName] as abap.numc(len)) as abap.int[int_len] ) as [FieldName]

I hope this helps you resolve the issue.

BR,
Elda

entry.title: Event Based Revenue Recognition - Sell from Stock (with POD)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We are reimplementing SAP S/4HANA 2023 (FPS 03) version, for event based revenue recognition (Sell from Stock scenario), to comply with the ASC 606/IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition requirements.

As Is Process:

Presently, for “Sell from Stock” scenario, we post PGI and billing on the same day to book the COGS and revenue in the same period.

This works in a normal scenario but in case of cut-off during month end processing, this process has limitations/constraints as described below:

Suppose there is a month end on Feb 28, 2025 and there are goods which have been delivered/shipped (PGI done) and billed (both on same day) on Feb 27, 2025.

Now, the goods are received by the customer on Mar 2, 2025 and not on Feb 28, 2025 which is the last day of period 2.

As per ASC 606, revenue should only be recognized once the goods have been physically delivered to the customer and there is a complete transfer of control over the goods from the seller to the customer.

In the above case, transfer of control is completed on Mar 2, 2025 but the COGS and revenue have already been booked in the earlier period (Period 2) which is not correct.

Ideally, revenue should be recognized on the physical delivery to the customer and COGS should be booked in the same period as revenue, to comply with the matching concept.

As a workaround, users now monitor the shipment to check when the goods are being received by the customer. If the physical delivery is in the next period as in the above example, Business is posting the adjustment in the earlier period (P2) to adjust the revenue and COGS and reverse the same on the first day of the next period (P3 in this case).

This is fine for month end processing, but revenue still remains in the earlier period (P2) after reversal of adjustment entries which doesn’t comply with ASC 606.

To Be Process:

We are looking for a solution based on “Event Based Revenue Recognition – Sell from stock” (with POD may be) which can take care of revenue recognition compliance with ASC 606.

We have explored this solution, and it looks like this could be used to resolve our revenue recognition issues.

We are expecting the below flow and postings at each event so that we can comply with the ASC 606 and e-invoicing requirements of LATAM countries:

PGI --> Billing (EBRR) --> POD

Event

GL Account

Dr/Cr

PGI

Deferred COGS (Bal sheet GL)

Dr

 

Stock

Cr

 

 

 

Billing

AR

Dr

 

Unbilled revenue

Cr

 

 

 

POD

COGS

Dr

 

Deferred COGS

Cr

 

 

 

 

Unbilled revenue

Dr

 

Revenue

Cr

 

There is another possible flow as below:

PGI -->  POD --> Billing (EBRR)

But as we have statutory requirement to generate e-invoice along with PGI itself because of which we can’t wait for billing until POD.

Impact on Actual Costing:

Another thing that we need to clarify is the impact of this setup on actual costing.

Right now, we have actual costing activated for all the company codes and we do actual costing revaluation as a part of month end process (CKMLCP) and also release the prices (CKME).

So, the COGS and inventory are revalued every month based on PUP.

The current setup for COGS revaluation is as below:

 

440700 - INVENTORY REVALUATION

However, we are not using regular COGS GL here in COC but it is a separate GL for inventory revaluation.

With EBRR activated as above, would there be any impact on actual costing setup as regular COGS will be hitting after POD confirmation and maybe in the next period in many cases?

Can you please clarify on the below questions:

  1. Can we use scope item 1K2 (EBRR – Sell from stock scenario) with POD confirmation to meet our requirement to comply with ASC 606?
  2. What will be its impact on actual costing revaluation as regular COGS will be hitting after POD confirmation?

entry.content:

entry.title: Re: transporting custom table data from DEV to QA and then to PROD
entry.content:
@sasidharConsidering the post you're commenting on is almost 20 years old, it would honestly be surprising if the link still worked. This post is older than iPhone. 🙂

There is, however, this blog post with screenshots and detailed instructions:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/step-by-step-guide-to-transport-table-contents-from-one-environment-to/ba-p/13240313

I found it using Google. Which still works since 2006.

entry.title: Multiple performs with tables calling same form
entry.content:
Hi,

I have 3 different internal tables with DIFFERENT structures with respective work areas (wa).
Say :
itab1, wa1
itab2, wa2
itab3, wa3.

These internal tables are appended based on different scenarios.

I want to build a common FORM subroutine to display the result of itabs via different performs  like below

perform assign_invoice tables itab1 using wa1.
perform assign_invoice tables itab2 using wa2.
perform assign_invoice tables itab3 using wa3.

form assign_invoice tables xtab using wa.
data: lv_opbel type opbel.

loop at xtab into wa.
lv_opbel = wa-opbel. -->errors out saying wa has NO structure & hence no OPBEL field.
endloop.

endform.

In debug mode, i can see wa having OPBEL. 

The actual  wa1 has opbel as part of the structure.

Can anyone suggest please.

entry.title: Re: Filter using Time Field
entry.content:
Maybe it's too late but I managed to work with an ODATA V2 like this:

$filter=CreationTime eq time'PT11H00M00S'

entry.title: SCIM API for User and Group Master Data (ARIBA) - Remove a user from all groups
entry.content: We’re currently running an offboarding process and I’d like to remove all groups associated with a user to ensure the license is no longer counted. I tried doing this in a single SCIM API call, but wasn’t successful. Do you know if this is possible?

entry.title: Automatic Workarea Migration / Activation of Redesigned Help Panel (v2411 on-premise)
entry.content:
We are in the early stages of upgrading to v2411. Our technical team upgraded our sandbox server and client tools on a couple of virtual machines for us to test on.

Workarea Migration

When we opened Producer, the Migrate Workarea dialog box didn’t appear as described in the upgrade guide at help.sap.com and Info Center instructions. The Workspace > Administration > Migrate Workarea option similarly was greyed out. Based on Google research, we realized this could be because the resources may have already been upgraded. Sure enough, the system log provided by our technical team (ref: 1st screen capture below) seemed to confirm our suspicions as did a scan of the change history resource-by-resource (ref: 2nd screen capture below). The last time we upgraded (to v2211), the workarea migration was not completed until we triggered it in Producer. This time, it appears to have happened automatically as part of the upgrade activities undertaken by our technical team.

System Log:

Resource Change History:

Is this change in behaviour due to our technical team selecting a different option to migrate the workareas automatically during their upgrade activities or is automatic migration now a default? In either case, is this process documented somewhere?  We didn’t see reference to such a change in the release notes since v2211 nor, as we said, in the documentation at help.sap.com or in the Info Center.

Redesigned Help Panel

Part of our upgrade testing includes opening the Web Assistant help panel from an S/4 HANA application. We were surprised to see the same old look and feel (no coloured buttons, no moveable panel – ref: screen capture below). Is there a setting in Producer or Manager that needs to be changed to activate the new panel? 

Any advise or assistance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

David Hortop

(He/Him)

Contractor, DRMIS Training Support, Director Defence Resource Management Information System / Digital Services Group

National Defence / Government of Canada

David.Hortop@forces.gc.ca / D365: david.hortop@ecn.forces.gc.ca

Obrigada,

After this task I have to sort out a load of lentils my stepmother has emptied into the cold ashes of our hearth.

entry.title: API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV to retrieve Purchase Orders
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using the API -API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV to retrieve all the purchase orders information from a S/4 HANA system. I get all the PO data along with PO quantity but I also would like to get the supplier confirmation quantity which is under 'Confirmations' tab in ME23N.

I tried using the entity A_PurchaseOrderScheduleLine but it wont return this field. This field exist in table EKET-DABMG.

Appreciate any inputs on this.

entry.title: Re: Error running step by step to generate ABAP Flight Reference Scenario tables
entry.content: Don't you see that /DMO/CL_FLIGHT_DATA_GENERATOR doesn't contain any code, nothing can be executed, so your question should be why you couldn't install the class from https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-refscen-flight/blob/ABAP-platform-2019/src/legacy/%23dmo%23cl_flight_data_generator.clas.abap

entry.title: Timestamp in SRT_MONI Reflecting When Message Was First Sent Outbound
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

I'm working with data in SRT_MONI and struggling to understand what is the exact field that identifies the moment in which the message is sent out bound.

What is the field that is giving me this information?
Or is there a way I can have that time by adding performance data to 

CREATED_TS

?

entry.title: Setting up SESSION_TIMEOUT_STOP_DATA_REFRESH
entry.content:
Hello Support,

 

We are facing an issue with the internal timeout setting for SESSION_TIMEOUT_STOP_DATA_REFRESH, which is currently set to 15 minutes. We would like to extend this timeout to 60 minutes.

 

How can we do this?

 

Kind regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Setting up SESSION_TIMEOUT_STOP_DATA_REFRESH
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

The parameter SESSION_TIMEOUT_STOP_DATA_REFRESH determines the time a user has to be inactive before the tiles stop sending polling data refresh requests. 

This timeout can be configured in Manage Launchpad Settings application. You can enter the time in minutes, or if you enter -1 you disable the timeout. 

For more information, check the following documentations:

  • Configuring the Automatic Sign-Out.
  • SAP Help Portal | Manage Launchpad Settings

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Add ALV on my report
entry.content: Search the forum CL_SALV_TABLE=>FACTORY( IMPORTING r_salv_table = o_alv CHANGING t_table = lt_result ) and the display O_ALV->DISPLAY( ). Be careful, the internal table must be declared globally or as a persistent class or instance attribute.

entry.title: Re: Multiple performs with tables calling same form
entry.content:
You didn't define a type for WA, hence the compiler doesn't know that it has the component OPBEL.

Example of fix:

ASSIGN COMPONENT 'OPBEL' OF STRUCTURE wa TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<opbel>).

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Alert Message & Message Definitions: Enhance User Experience
entry.content: No longer works with the new global benefits open enrollment experience 2024.
